rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM BROOKS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JULY 21, 2008, LINE 1, DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT RELIES ON INADMISSIBLE HEARSAY DECLARATIONS OF JOHN MEYERS TO SUPPORT THE MOTION. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO THIS EVIDENCE IS SUSTAINED. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM BROOKS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE KING et al VS. AC AND S,INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Written Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,520.00
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JULY 21, 2008, LINE 2, DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Responses To Written Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,520.00 IS OFF CALENDAR, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED ON JULY 10, 2008. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE KING et al VS. AC AND S,INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JULY 21, 2008, LINE 2, DEFENDANT AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE BASED ON FACTUALLY DEVOID DISCOVERY. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE FROM WHICH A TRIER OF FACT COULD CONCLUDE THAT DECEDENT WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS FROM PRODUCTS MANUFACTURED OR SUPPLIED BY DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE THAT DECEDENT HAD CONTACT WITH DEFENDANT'S VEHICLES, BOTH NEW AND USED, AND DEFENDANT'S REPLACEMENT PARTS. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN THAT AT TIME OF THE CONTACT THE PRODUCTS CONTAINED ASBESTOS, THAT SUCH ASBESTOS WAS RELEASED AROUND DECEDENT IN ANY OR SUFFICIENT QUANTITIES TO CAUSE THE DISEASE. =(302/PJM/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: ONE CLARENCE PLACE OWNERS ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNI VS. MARTIN PROPERTIES, LLC et al

rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JULY 21, 2008, LINE 3, CROSS-DEFENDANT GOLD RIVER CONTRACTORS Motion For Recover Of Attorney's Fees And Costs From Cross-Complainant Martin Mcnerney Development, Inc. rulings.Case Title: SURE BUILDING PRODUCTS, INC. et al VS. TCC STEEL, A KOREAN COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JULY 21, 2008, LINE 4, PLAINTIFFS SURE BUILDING PRODUCTS, INC., DELTA BUILDING PRODUCTS, LTD., AND OMEGA ROOF, INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. THE MOTION TO QUASH IS DENIED. THE MOVING PARTY HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE PLEADING IS A SHAM. BASED ON THE FILING OF THIS AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT BELIEVES THAT THE PRIOR ORDER OF CONSOLIDATION MUST BE RE-EXAMINED FOR IT NO LONGER APPEARS THAT CONSOLIDATION WILL RESULT IN THE EFFICIENT RESOLUTION OF THE CORE DISPUTE WHICH IS SET FORTH IN SURE BUILDING PRODUCTS' COMPLAINT. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: METROPOLIS PARTNERS, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. 365 MAIN INCORPORATED, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JULY 21, 2008, LINE 8, DEFENDANTS 365 MAIN INCORPORATED AND CHRISTOPHER DOLAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. HEARING REQUIRED ON THE PARTIES' EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS. THE PARTIES ARE TO FOCUS ON THE OBJECTIONS AND ADDRESS ONLY THE OBJECTIONS THAT WARRANT ORAL ARGUMENT. AFTER THE EVIDENTIARY RECORD IS SET, THE COURT WILL HOLD A FURTHER HEARING AT A SUBSEQUENT DATE TO ADDRESS THE MERITS OF THE MOTION. THE FOLLOWING ARE THE COURT'S PRELIMINARY OBSERVATIONS OF THE ISSUES PRESENTED: 1. THE RELEVANT LANGUAGE OF THE MOU IS NOT AMBIGUOUS. 2. THE DECISION IN COPELAND V. BASKIN ROBINS USA (2002) 96 CAL.APP. 4TH 1251 IS PERSUASIVE AUTHORITY ON THE ISSUE OF LOST PROFITS. 3. DEFENDANTS ARGUMENT ON DAMAGES IS CONFUSING. 4. DEFENDANTS HAD CONTACTS WITH ROCKWOOD BEFORE TERMINATING THE MOU. 5. THE FACTS RELEVANT TO ISSUES 3 AND 4 CREATE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO EACH CAUSE OF ACTION. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE E PORTEOUS et al VS. LELAND F PORTEOUS et al

rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JULY 21, 2008, LINE 9, PLAINTIFFS LAWRENCE E. PORTEOUS AND LAWRENCE E. PORTEOUS, D.D.S., INC.'S Motion To Convert August 10, 2007 Judgment To Money Judgment IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE GIVEN THE PENDING APPEAL, THE COURT LACKS JURISDICTION TO CONSIDER THE MOTION. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES SCHWAB & CO VS. LARRY YU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JULY 21, 2008, LINE 10, DEFENDANTS LARRY YU, CARLY FONG, PHILLIP SHINN, AND THORTON, TAYLOR, BECKER & SHINN'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES. FEES ARE AWARDED IN THE AMOUNT OF $21,277.50. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE WORK ON THE SUBPOENAS AND THE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WAS INTERTWINED WITH THE ANTI-SLAPP ISSUES AND WAS NECESSARY FOR THE MOVING PARTIES TO SUCCESSFULLY LITIGATE THAT ISSUE. THE SUM OF $2,975.00 IS INCLUDED FOR WORK IN CONNECTION WITH THE FEE MOTION. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: DON KEANE VS. ABSOLUTELYNEW, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JULY 21, 2008, LINE 13, DEFENDANT ABSOLUTELYNEW, INC.'S Petition To Compel Arbitration IS GRANTED. IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT THE PARTIES ENTERED INTO AN ENFORCEABLE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT. IT ALSO APPEARS THAT BOTH PARTIES, TO DATE, HAVE FAILED TO COMPLY WITH ALL OF THE REQUIREMENTS TO COMMENCE THE ARBITRATION. HOWEVER, SUCH CONDUCT IS NOT A BASIS FOR CONCLUDING THAT THERE HAS BEEN A WAIVER, PARTICULARLY GIVEN THE LANGUAGE OF THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT REQUIRING THAT ANY WAIVER BE IN WRITING. THE POLICY OF THIS STATE FAVORS ARBITRATION WHERE THE PARTIES HAVE AGREED TO DO SO. =(302/PJM/AH-X)
